objective: Require consumer verifier commands and evidence before Worker handoff acceptance when consumer-managed governance surfaces are touched.
scope_boundary: Extend existing consumer verifier, handoff package, execution-scope, and Worker route validators in place.
Add focused regression tests for governance-root typos, malformed local_overrides, stale workflow SHA, missing verifier evidence, and unsafe handoff refs.
Record governance evidence for issue #537 under the session guardrail reliability epic.
out_of_scope: Building a new handoff schema version.
research_summary: Issue #537 is open and unclaimed. Existing verifier code already checks local_overrides and reusable workflow SHAs, but --governance-root did not drive default manifest resolution and accepted handoff packages did not require consumer verifier commands/evidence when consumer-managed paths were in scope. Existing execution-scope and Worker route validators can consume stricter evidence-ref normalization without adding a parallel gate.

sprints.name: Consumer verifier and Worker acceptance gate
sprints.goal: Prevent accepted Worker handoffs for consumer-managed governance changes unless verifier commands and evidence refs are present.
tasks: Resolve consumer verifier defaults from --governance-root and fail typoed roots.
Add stale workflow SHA fixture coverage.
Require consumer verifier command and evidence refs for accepted handoffs touching consumer-managed paths.
Normalize handoff evidence refs through safe repo-relative path validation.
Prove Worker route blocks unsafe evidence refs.
acceptance_criteria: Typoed or missing governance roots fail the consumer verifier.
Malformed local_overrides fails with direct regression coverage.
Stale or mismatched governance workflow SHA remains a hard failure.
Accepted handoffs touching consumer-managed paths require verify_governance_consumer.py commands.
Accepted handoffs touching consumer-managed paths require verifier evidence refs.
Worker acceptance cannot proceed with unsafe handoff evidence refs.

specialist_reviews.focus: Existing acceptance gate and consumer verifier contracts
specialist_reviews.summary: The implementation updates existing validators and focused tests without introducing downstream repo writes.

alternate_model_review.summary: Bounded Tier 2 guardrail repair with deterministic tests; no new standard or cross-family plan review required.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Implementation may update consumer verifier, handoff package validation, execution-scope validation, Worker route tests, and issue #537 governance evidence only.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Run focused tests, governance validators, local CI gate, then open and merge PR after green checks.
handoff_acceptance_criteria: Focused verifier and handoff tests pass.
Implementation stays inside allowed write paths.
Stage 6 closeout and self-learning evidence are present.

material_deviation_rules: If downstream repo edits are needed, stop and open repo-specific follow-up issues.
If package-handoff schema changes are required, split to a schema-versioned issue.
If consumer workflow classification needs per-repo policy, record it as downstream rollout scope.
